title: BEM-926 worker deep review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM926_WORKER_DEEP_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-926 WORKER DEEP PROTOCOL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_MANDATORY_CORRECTIONS
Operator correction:
- Previous BEM-925 still failed because it treated objects as if they had prompts.
- Objects such as General Director, Director and Worker are not LLM agents. They must have object passports, rules, functions, responsibilities, channels and lifecycle, not prompts.
- Prompts belong only to LLM-executed roles/providers: GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor, Claude Executor, GPT-Codex fallback roles, and possibly operational handler contracts.
Mandatory structure for new protocol:
1. Russian-first language. English only for technical identifiers in parentheses.
2. Executive diagnosis: current system is governance scaffold plus partial deterministic baseline, not working managing contour yet.
3. Clear separation:
   - Object passports: GD, Director, Worker.
   - Element registry: Curators, Analysts, Auditors, Executors.
   - Contour registry: static/dynamic contours, workspaces and properties.
   - Provider registry: GPT, Claude, GPT-Codex/Codex.
   - Prompt pack: only for LLM roles/providers and operational handlers.
4. Detailed creation roadmap must explicitly create/implement General Director, Director and Worker as objects in the repository, including passport, registry refs, channels, curators, contours, reports and tests.
5. Inner contour algorithm must be exact: Curator -> Analyst; Analyst prepares proposal; Auditor approves or rejects proposal; if approved, Executor executes; Executor returns result to Auditor; Auditor approves result or sends revision through Analyst; loop repeats until auditor-approved result.
6. Vertical links are Curator-to-Curator. Horizontal links are Auditor of contour A -> Analyst of contour B, or Analyst B requests verified data from Auditor A.
7. Workspace belongs to internal contour, not element. Main workspace is approved repository code/state.
8. No local element rules by default. Curator inherits system/object/channel rules; contour elements inherit system/object/contour/properties/experience.
9. Provider model must be part of execution lifecycle, not standalone: primary GPT Analyst + Claude Auditor + Claude Executor; fallback GPT-Codex/Codex for Claude roles on limits/unavailability.
10. Existing mechanisms must be used: ai-devops-telegram-curator-webhook, POST / Telegram webhook, /codex-task, /codex-status, codex-runner.yml. /autonomy is support. /gpt-safe is legacy compatibility.
11. Claude requirements are mandatory acceptance criteria.
12. Roadmap must be detailed into many substeps, not eight vague lines. Each phase must contain artifacts, exact files, checks, proof, status and gate.
Required final document sections:
- Purpose and mistakes corrected.
- What exists now / what is missing / what blocks working state.
- Target working architecture.
- Object passports (GD, Director, Worker), not object prompts.
- Elements and role templates.
- Internal contours, static/dynamic, workspaces.
- Links and channels mechanics.
- Provider model and failover.
- Telegram and ChatGPT operator fronts.
- GitHub Actions and runners as activation/proof mechanisms.
- SSOT registries and schemas.
- Prompt pack for LLM/provider roles only.
- Detailed roadmap, at least 12 phases with substeps.
- Acceptance criteria and Definition of Done.
- Operator gates and secret names only.
Status recommendation:
- PREPARE_DOCX_FOR_OPERATOR_REVIEW_AFTER_CORRECTION.
No issue comments.
